result:
The refactor is complete and verified. Summary of changes:

**`reportgen/config.py`** — Replaced the `DEFAULTS` dict with a frozen dataclass `Config` carrying the four typed fields and identical defaults (`title="Report"`, `output_format="text"`, `max_rows=10`, `include_summary=True`). `load_config` now validates every JSON key against the dataclass field names — raising `ValueError` naming any unknown key — then constructs a `Config(**raw)`, so file values still override defaults.

**`reportgen/formatter.py`** — Switched all `config[...]` lookups to attribute access (`config.output_format`, `config.title`, `config.include_summary`).

**`reportgen/loader.py`** — `config["max_rows"]` → `config.max_rows`.

`main.py` and `__init__.py` needed no changes (they never subscripted the config).

Verification:
- `python3 -m pytest -q` → **4 passed**, tests untouched.
- `grep` confirms no `cfg[...]`/`config[...]` subscripting remains in `reportgen/`.
- Manual checks confirm correct defaults, override behavior, frozen immutability (`FrozenInstanceError`), and the new `ValueError` on unknown keys.

No git commits were made and all work stayed inside the current directory.
